threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look baffled when the bus driver yells at you for not having an Oyster card ready. The locals just flash it without a glance.
Last night on the 35, a woman was singing Marvin Gaye at the back, while the driver pretended not to notice. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old fridge as a community library in the park. That quirky creativity feels so Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that doubles as a hamster café. Sip your drink while tiny furballs scurry around—definitely quirky!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the smell of a burnt burrito from El Farolito means the night just got real, and it's not even midnight yet.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today with a dog in a tutu and a guy playing classical guitar. Only in the Tenderloin, right? A real slice of daily life.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists snap photos of Casa Ramos, but overlook the little green grocer on Carrer de l'Astúries—where the seasonal fruits are fresher than any fancy market.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones struggling with the public transport ticket machines, pressing buttons like they’re defusing a bomb.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You don't yet know the 'secret' shortcuts through the backstreets. You'll still be on the main roads while locals glide past, grinning.
Heard a guy on the N37 bus bragging about faking a phone call to avoid a drunk chap trying to sit next to him. Classic Brixton night antics.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sipping a coffee on Hermannplatz, I watched a kid juggle oranges while a busker played the accordion so out of tune it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club that doubles as a skate park. It’s the perfect blend of nightlife and daytime adrenaline, all under one roof.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they instinctively know which taco truck is worth the wait at 3 AM, not just the one with the longest line.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, a guy on the 47 bus serenaded us with a ukulele while we waited for the next stop. Classic Muni, unexpected and bizarre.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí. They miss the real gems hidden in the little plazas, where the locals actually play petanca.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewildered faces at the Palau de la Música; they’re the ones trying to read the schedule and ending up in the wrong place.
